evals.prompt: Set up my resume workspace. I have a LaTeX template in ~/templates/awesome-cv/
evals.expected_output: Creates material/ directory with all .md files from scaffolding, reads and analyzes the template at ~/templates/awesome-cv/, detects .tex/.cls files and compiler, saves config to .claude/resume-config.json, creates docs/resumes/, suggests .gitignore entries

evals.prompt: Initialize my resume. I want to clone my Overleaf template: https://www.overleaf.com/project/abc123def456
evals.expected_output: Creates material/ directory, converts Overleaf URL to git URL (https://git.overleaf.com/abc123def456), clones into templates/, analyzes the cloned template structure, saves config with source: overleaf and source_url

Checks for existing .claude/resume-config.json (fresh setup if missing), creates material/ with scaffolding files, prompts for template source (local/Overleaf/GitHub), guides through material collection options (interactive/self-guided/import)
evals.notes: Minimal input — skill should guide the user through the full setup interactively

evals.prompt: I already ran setup before. I want to update my template from Overleaf.
evals.expected_output: Detects existing .claude/resume-config.json, reads it, offers reconfigure/update/keep options. If user chooses update, re-pulls from the stored source_url without touching material/ files
evals.notes: Edge case: existing configuration with update request

evals.prompt: Setup resume. My template is at /nonexistent/path/template/
evals.expected_output: Attempts to validate the path, finds it doesn't exist or contains no .tex files, informs the user the path is invalid, asks for a corrected path. Does NOT save an invalid config.
